source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / curated learning principle
description: AROMER learns only from episodes that have been curated or verified. Raw unverified feedback from agents can be adversarial. Garbage-in, garbage-out is amplified in governance systems.

curated_sources: Shadow mode episodes with confirmed outcome
forbidden_sources: LLM-generated synthetic episodes without human audit
Episodes where outcome is unknown or unresolved
Episodes from adversarial test inputs used for evaluation
payload.aromer_constraint: Only episodes with ground_truth != null may update DomainHarmPrior. Others stored but not used for world model updates.

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / conservative learning
description: AROMER only commits a world model update when the update shows verified positive gain: better accuracy, lower false_accept, or lower calibration error on held-out episodes.

gain_verification.holdout_split: 20% of curated episodes held out for evaluation

payload.aromer_constraint: World model rollback if positive_verified_gain not confirmed after 100 episodes post-update
payload.governance_note: This law prevents adversarial learning: an attacker cannot gradually shift priors without triggering the gain verification check

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / safety learning boundary
description: AROMER never learns to weaken, circumvent, or reduce the effect of safety invariants or REMORA's core policy rules. The safety core is outside the learning boundary.

immutable_elements: Escalation logic for inv_no_mass_destruction and inv_no_credential_exfiltration
payload.detection: If a world_model update would result in safety invariant triggering <5% less often → reject update
payload.aromer_constraint: inv_no_policy_core_mutation applies to AROMER itself — AROMER cannot learn to avoid triggering its own invariants

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / conservative update law
description: Large changes to the world model require proportionally more evidence. Small adjustments can be made with 10 episodes; shifting a prior by >0.3 requires 100+ episodes.

payload.aromer_constraint: If proposed update exceeds evidence table → defer update, continue shadow collection
payload.rationale: Large rapid prior shifts are the fingerprint of data poisoning attacks

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / contrastive learning
description: False accepts and incorrect blocks are more informative per episode than correct decisions. AROMER over-weights negative examples to avoid availability bias from a sea of routine correct accepts.

payload.application: When computing world model updates, multiply episode weight by decision_quality weight from this table
payload.rationale: Prevents the model from being overwhelmed by low-signal routine accepts and missing the high-signal failure cases
payload.alignment_with_code: Maps to DecisionQuality.world_model_weight property in remora/aromer/experience/episode.py
